title: Multiparameter function estimation for general Hamiltonians
authors: Erfan Abbasgholinejad, Sean R. Muleady, Jacob Bringewatt, Lorcan O. Conlon, Alexey V. Gorshkov
abstract: Estimation of physical parameters encoded in a Hamiltonian is a central task in quantum sensing and learning. While the ultimate precision limit for estimating a single parameter coupled to a single generator is well established, the corresponding bound for estimating a function of multiple parameters-each coupled to distinct and possibly non-commuting generators-remains unknown in general. Here, we derive the ultimate quantum limit and present an estimation protocol for any function of parameters in a general Hamiltonian that attains this bound. We show that, although the task is fundamentally a multiparameter problem, our tight bound reduces to an optimized single-parameter quantum Cramér-Rao bound, even for arbitrary generator sets. Our result unifies and extends previous works, providing a general framework for optimal function estimation in quantum systems.
summary: This paper solves the problem of finding the fundamental quantum limit for estimating functions of multiple parameters in a general Hamiltonian. It proves that this complex task can be reduced to an optimized single-parameter problem and provides a practical, two-step adaptive protocol to reach this limit. This unifies previous results and extends them to the most general case of non-commuting generators.
main_problem: Determining the ultimate quantum precision limit for estimating a scalar function of multiple unknown parameters encoded in a general, potentially non-commuting Hamiltonian.
main_result: The authors derive a tight, saturable lower bound on the variance of the estimator that reduces the complex multi-parameter function estimation problem to an optimized single-parameter quantum Cramér-Rao bound.
method: The problem is mapped to an effective single-parameter estimation problem using a semidefinite programming (SDP) framework and a two-step adaptive protocol involving Hamiltonian reshaping and randomized Trotterization.
model_or_system: A general time-independent signal Hamiltonian H0 = sum f_j(theta) g_j, where theta are unknown parameters, f_j are smooth functions, and g_j are linearly independent, traceless Hermitian generators, supplemented by a controllable, time-dependent control Hamiltonian.
key_observables: The variance of the estimator for the target function q(theta).
important_parameters: The precision parameter gamma (derived from an SDP), the total interrogation time t, the Jacobian matrix F of the couplings, and the conditioning constant kappa.
main_assumptions: Parameters are time-independent, functions are twice continuously differentiable, and generators are linearly independent and traceless; the protocol assumes an asymptotic limit of large total time t.
figures_summary: Table I compares optimal precision bounds across various scenarios including single-parameter, commuting, and arbitrary generator sets.
paper_structure: The paper defines the multi-parameter function estimation problem, derives a tight lower bound via SDP duality, proposes a constructive protocol using Hamiltonian reshaping and adaptive estimation, and provides error analysis and stability theorems.
why_interesting: This work provides a fundamental framework for quantum sensing, offering a way to achieve optimal precision when dealing with complex, non-commuting signal generators, which is highly relevant for many-body sensing and quantum metrology.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
